candidate_policies.future_builder_contract: Spend charged grammar bytes first around VP and horizon boundaries; all learned tables and selected atoms must be archive members.
Track births/deaths over adjacent contest pair frames, then lower to charged component tracks or residual tiles.
Exact-copy stream chunks are byte-custody anchors only; a promoted grammar must replace them with charged decodeable atoms.
Promote stable high-density components into charged templates, with residual tiles for boundaries and class transitions.
Use spans for irregular mask areas that are cheaper than spline or component-track descriptions.
Use class support atoms as charged coarse masks before boundary and residual refinement.
Boundary atoms must lower to charged edge runs or templates inside the archive, never scorer-derived side information.

future_submission_sidecar_policy.note: Any future grammar table, selector, codebook, residual, or learned parameter must be serialized inside archive.zip.

mask_decode_contract.contract: The tensor was decoded only from charged extracted mask bytes via submissions/robust_current/inflate_renderer.py::_load_masks_from_archive; no scorer path, external sidecar, or score claim is involved.

non_promotable_reason: Planning-only atom table: no grammar archive was built, no decoder was integrated, and no CUDA auth eval was run on exact archive bytes.

planning_schema.byte_cost_units: estimated charged bytes before entropy coding

determinism.sort_keys: priority_density desc, then stable identity fields

required_score_truth: No score truth is produced here. A future full archive must run archive.zip -> inflate.sh -> upstream/evaluate.py via experiments/contest_auth_eval.py --device cuda.

build_contract.score_truth_required: archive.zip -> inflate.sh -> upstream/evaluate.py via experiments/contest_auth_eval.py --device cuda

charged_side_info_accounting.statement: All grammar headers, codebooks, model tables, selected atom ids, and residual bytes are charged archive payload estimates. Selector training weights are compress-time-only and are not required at inflate time.

candidate_specs.why_higher_ev_than_naive_crf_or_rpk1: allocates scarce mask bytes to traced PoseNet/SegNet hard pairs, horizon/vanishing-point/boundary atoms, and charged side info; naive CRF/RPK1 changed broad mask bytes without this component-aware trust region.

build_contract.score_truth_required: archive.zip -> inflate.sh -> upstream/evaluate.py via experiments/contest_auth_eval.py --device cuda
